snippet.title: Biotechnology and Healthcare: A New Dawn? With Bob Bradway, CEO of Amgen
snippet.description:
The late Steve Jobs of Apple said “I think the biggest innovation of the 21st century will be at the intersection of biology and technology, a new era is beginning”.

If you want to see how biotech can change the world of health, whilst potentially being capable of creating extraordinary value for investors, there are few better places to look than Amgen, one of the world’s leading independent biotech companies. With a market capitalization of approximately $130bn, Amgen has been an extraordinary success story.

In this conversation, Bob discusses the big areas of medical need, from cardiovascular problems which continue to make it the world’s greatest killer, through to genetics, obesity, osteoporosis and cancers.

Bob discusses timelines and priorities, the use of A.I. in accelerating drug development, biologics, facing setbacks on the road to approvals, and the challenge of running a company which has a presence in over 100 countries.

07:08 - The Difference Between Biotech & Pharmaceuticals

snippet.title: How Amgen is using Generative Biology and AI
snippet.description:
Amgen is featured in the Irish Pharmaceutical Healthcare Association's new 2023 "Innovate for Life" film series which dives into the social and economic value of medicines innovation. 

Watch and learn how Amgen is using advances in generative biology and AI to design novel protein therapeutics. Generative Biology is a game-changer with the potential to dramatically shorten the time it takes to develop drugs, reducing costs and getting them to patients who need them most, faster.

Amgen Dun Laoghaire (near Dublin, Ireland) plays a significant role in Amgen's global manufacturing and supply chain infrastructure. 

#generativebiology #amgen #AI #TransformTomorrow

snippet.title: Helping Institutional Investors Invest With Confidence - With Jo Holden
snippet.description:
In our recent discussions with some of the world’s super allocators, we have heard about their investment objectives and varying approaches to asset allocation.

In this episode, Joanne Holden, Mercer's Global Head of Investment Research & Consulting, explains why a fascination with maths led her to actuarial work and then to investment consulting, and working for over two decades at Mercer.

She explains why investing institutions need consultants, how they use them, and how they approach the world of asset allocation and manager selection.

She also discusses, the issues around sustainability, manager analysis, and more broadly glass ceilings, resilience, and advice for young people thinking about finance.

snippet.title: Wendell Pierce Breaks Character in Broadway Play & Confronts Unruly Guest
snippet.description:
Actor Wendell Pierce found himself having to break character and temporarily pause a recent Broadway production of ‘Death of a Salesman’ to address an unruly patron in the front rows.

According to several reports, the audience member began causing a disturbance in the play’s middle act, to the point where security had to be called to escort them out. Cellphone footage from the theater shows Pierce, who plays the lead role of salesman Willy Loman, attempting to calm the individual.

‘He patiently & heroically pleaded with her to leave peacefully despite her insistence that she should be carried out forcefully (she was eventually escorted out by police),’ said one audience member, via Instagram.

snippet.title: Judy Garland as a Blackface Pickaninny in Everybody Sing
snippet.description:
#blackface #racism #minstrelshow
Judy Garland performs Swing low, Sweet Chariot as a Blackface pickaninny in Everybody Sing. Judy performed in blackface in three movies during her career; Everybody Sing (1938) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y9glvXLW1tw , Babes in Arms (1939) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=epVUXREs6X8 and Babes on Broadway (1941) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EUfLbIs309E . True fans should love Judy Garland for everything else she did and not try to defend her for doing this by claiming she had no agency or sense of what was right and wrong for that time. On the other extreme, condemning her for doing it seems a bit excessive as well, when one takes everything known about her into consideration. Like all history, it's complicated.
Read about the history of blackface and minstrel shows at: http://www.black-face.com

snippet.title: Updated analysis of the MAIA trial: long-term safety and efficacy data
snippet.description:
Supratik Basu, MBBS, MRCP, FRCPath, FRCP, The Royal Wolverhampton Hospital NHS Trust, Wolverhampton, UK, shares an update on the Phase III MAIA trial (NCT02252172), which is evaluating the safety and efficacy of adding daratumumab to lenalidomide and dexamethasone in patients with transplant-ineligible multiple myeloma. Long-term follow-up data demonstrate that there is a significant progression-free survival and overall survival (PFS; OS) advantage in patients receiving the daratumumab-containing regimen, confirming the well established role of this agent in the upfront and relapsed settings. This interview took place at the 63rd Annual Scientific Meeting of the British Society for Haematology (BSH) 2023, held in Birmingham, UK.

snippet.title: Career Profile: Danielle Ernst, Amgen Thousand Oaks
snippet.description:
Danielle Ernest thinks of herself as a forecaster—of social media. Her job as a senior manager in corporate affairs at Amgen is to manage and develop the company's social media platforms with the goal of being a steward for the brand. "My job focuses on building reputation and brand awareness on social media," she explains. "I am always looking ahead and down the tracks...looking to see what's trending, where it's going...and the next steps of where social is going to go." In this new video profile, learn about Ernest's path from Disney to Amgen and more details about her job in biotechnology.

The Amgen Biotech Experience (ABE) is an innovative science education program that empowers teachers to bring biotechnology to their classrooms. ABE provides teacher professional development, curriculum materials, and research-grade equipment and supplies to secondary schools.

Learn more at https://www.amgenbiotechexperience.com.

snippet.title: How Technology is Accelerating & Democratising Private Markets – With Matt Brown
snippet.description:
In this conversation we discuss both the changing nature of demand for alternative assets as well as their supply.

Matt explains how CAIS is creating the bridge between the alternative investment firms and the wealth management community, whose portfolio allocations to these investments are a fraction of their institutional counterparts.

Matt discusses the size of the market opportunity, how technology is enabling this rapid transition, and helping solve the problem of unequal access to “alternatives”.

He talks about branding within this space, competition, regulation and how Mercer are providing core due diligence to introduce independence to the arena.

The discussion then moves to how investors are re-appraising the traditional 60/40 allocation to bonds and stocks, to incorporate a wider portfolio including alternative investments.

Subscribe to stay up to date with our range of fascinating interviews, featuring some of the biggest names in global finance and business!

localized.title: How Technology is Accelerating & Democratising Private Markets – With Matt Brown
localized.description:
In this conversation we discuss both the changing nature of demand for alternative assets as well as their supply.

Matt explains how CAIS is creating the bridge between the alternative investment firms and the wealth management community, whose portfolio allocations to these investments are a fraction of their institutional counterparts.

Matt discusses the size of the market opportunity, how technology is enabling this rapid transition, and helping solve the problem of unequal access to “alternatives”.

He talks about branding within this space, competition, regulation and how Mercer are providing core due diligence to introduce independence to the arena.

The discussion then moves to how investors are re-appraising the traditional 60/40 allocation to bonds and stocks, to incorporate a wider portfolio including alternative investments.

Aubrey De Grey, a scientist working on developing medical innovations that can postpone age-related illnesses. The interview discusses the Longevity Escape Velocity (LEV) Foundation and their work on mouse studies, Yamanaka factors, and more. The video also talks about the challenges faced by scientists working on anti-aging medicine and how these challenges have been overcome over the years through increased funding and investor interest. The video also covers De Grey's prediction that within 15 years, there is a 50% chance that we would be out of the "death spiral" of longevity.


In the full interview we get into:
Mark Hamaleinen write in question: Why we still don't have any therapies based on his ideas published 20 years ago in his breakout paper
Update on escape velocity. 50% in 15 years.
Experiments on mice
Yamanaka factor thoughts, current research on mice continued
Lev foundation research expanded and explained
Lev foundation thesis compared to others
Hardships being at the tip of the innovation field of longevity

Next-generation mentorship

snippet.title: De que é feita uma boa liderança? | Human Forward Talks
snippet.description:
Num mundo do trabalho em constante mudança, em que tudo é cada vez mais ágil e rápido, a única certeza é ter de lidar com a incerteza. Nos últimos anos, muitos profissionais passaram a olhar de outra forma para a carreira e vida profissional. Hoje existe uma maior preocupação com o bem-estar, com a conciliação entre a vida profissional e vida pessoal e com o próprio desenvolvimento. É, por isso, uma nova realidade que implica também uma nova dinâmica na gestão. O foco deixou de estar apenas centrado no negócio para estar igualmente centrado nas pessoas. Quais são, por isso, os desafios que as lideranças de hoje enfrentam? Esta é a questão central do novo episódio da série “Human Forward Talks”. Erica Pereira, Diretora da Randstad Professionals em Portugal, Catarina Tendeiro, Diretora de Recursos Humanos da Hovione Portugal, e Sofia Brazão, Diretora de Recursos Humanos na Amgen Portugal, partilham a sua visão sobre como deve ser a feita a gestão de pessoas num mercado em profunda transformação e qual deve o papel do líder na atração e retenção de talento. 

Site: https://www.brandscommunity.pt/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/Bbrandschannel
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/brandschannel/
Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/64cbc4Kt8G4EbXvERg1dgk
